description: CI gate for the #1833 BM25-only replay fixture. Baseline and candidate run the same fixed profile over the same corpus, so any ranking movement is unintended drift: every verdict class except 'unchanged' is capped at 0, and each metric aggregate delta must not drop below 0. corpus_match / index_match are intentionally NOT required — both fold wall-clock timestamps and machine-hashed paths into their fingerprints, so a committed baseline can never match a freshly indexed candidate (see README). Labels are complete over the corpus (target-file chunks relevant, every other corpus chunk not_relevant), so precision is comparable and stable for every case; the precision floor then catches a same-corpus candidate that appends an off-target (labelled not_relevant) result after the rank-1 hit — rank metrics stay flat but precision drops. max_incomplete_precision_cases=0 closes the complementary gap (#1837): a corpus change that makes a case retrieve an unlabelled chunk would otherwise drop that case from the precision cohort and slip through as unchanged, so any compared case with incomplete precision is now a violation rather than a silent exclusion.
